verses.text: David cries to the LORD his rock not to be silent, lifting his hands toward the holy place. He dreads God's silence above all. To be unheard by God is the one thing the praying soul cannot bear.
David cries to the LORD his rock not to be silent, lifting his hands toward the holy place. He dreads God's silence above all. To be unheard by God is the one thing the praying soul cannot bear.

He asks not to be dragged off with the wicked, whose hearts are full of mischief, and leaves their just reward to God. He will not share their way or their doom. Those who disregard God's works invite the judgment they ignore.

He asks not to be dragged off with the wicked, whose hearts are full of mischief, and leaves their just reward to God. He will not share their way or their doom. Those who disregard God's works invite the judgment they ignore.

He asks not to be dragged off with the wicked, whose hearts are full of mischief, and leaves their just reward to God. He will not share their way or their doom. Those who disregard God's works invite the judgment they ignore.

'Blessed be the LORD, because he hath heard,' David cries; 'the LORD is my strength and my shield... my heart greatly rejoiceth.' Answered prayer turns to song. The trusting heart that is helped cannot keep from praising.

'Blessed be the LORD, because he hath heard,' David cries; 'the LORD is my strength and my shield... my heart greatly rejoiceth.' Answered prayer turns to song. The trusting heart that is helped cannot keep from praising.

The LORD is the strength of His people; David prays, 'Save thy people... feed them also, and lift them up for ever.' His joy widens into intercession. The God who shepherds us is asked to shepherd all His flock.

The LORD is the strength of His people; David prays, 'Save thy people... feed them also, and lift them up for ever.' His joy widens into intercession. The God who shepherds us is asked to shepherd all His flock.
